summary: Addresses external review #2 feedback: (1) Reorders reconcile steps so config generation runs before database and bootstrap, since both Jobs need the keystone.conf ConfigMap for DB connection; (2) Adds config ConfigMap volume mounts to db_sync and bootstrap Job specs, matching the Deployment pattern. All tests updated to pass configMapName through the call chain, with new assertions verifying volume mounts on both Jobs.

tests_checklist.item: New volume mount assertions added for db_sync Job (reconcile_database_test.go:335-345)
New volume mount assertions added for bootstrap Job (reconcile_bootstrap_test.go:159-168)
testComputeConfigMapName helper computes deterministic name for integration tests
Sequential progression test updated with correct prerequisites for new ordering
Early-return-on-database-not-ready test updated to include fernet keys and DB credentials
Edge cases covered: stale job detection still works with configMapName parameter

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows sub-reconciler condition contract pattern with ObservedGeneration
Volume mount structure consistent across Deployment, db_sync Job, and bootstrap Job
No dead code, no scope creep
Functions remain focused — configMapName passed as parameter, not stored as state

security_checklist.item: No hardcoded secrets — credentials still sourced from SecretKeyRef
No new input validation needed — configMapName is internally generated

architecture_checklist.item: Reconcile ordering matches data dependencies (config before db_sync and bootstrap)
configMapName flows through call chain without global state
Both Jobs mount only what they need (config only, not fernet/credential keys)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — volume mount construction inline in each builder is appropriate (different Jobs)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Config errors propagated before database or bootstrap Jobs can run
Sub-reconcilers still return early with requeue on non-ready state

defensive_checklist.item: reconcileConfig failure prevents db_sync and bootstrap from running with missing config
Volume mount uses ReadOnly to prevent accidental config mutation
